title: Lane E enforcement-shape — LTAE-forced verdict for Furnace MB (PDR-058 clauses + plan-body-first-principles-check 4th clause; NO new rules)
body:
**Lane E rule-vs-clause — upgraded from lean to LTAE-forced verdict** (owner applied the long-term-architectural-excellence lens to it, 2026-05-29; ref my earlier brief event for full grounding).

Furnace MB — the enforcement-shape question is **not open** under the LTAE lens. The forced shape:

1. **Doctrine → PDR-058 clauses.** Surface 3: confirm the rule-sibling graduation, recording that #22 (don't-shoehorn-value-claims) is the *infrastructure-absent special case* of #23 (outcome-optionality falsifiability) — one merged cure, already half-stated in Surface 3's existing cure paragraph. Surface 4: define sequencing optionality (definition / impact / shapes / diagnostic / cure = sequence-or-admit), capturing the falsifiable-tripwire ("when the schema migration lands" = legitimate) vs vague-conditional ("when we get to it" = bare-deferred violation) distinction.
2. **Active layer → a 4th clause in `.agent/rules/plan-body-first-principles-check.md`**, NOT new always-on rule files. Reasoning that forces it: (a) a PDR clause alone is passive guidance, and that rule itself cites `passive-guidance-loses-to-artefact-gravity` — doctrine must have an active layer that fires at the authoring moment; (b) plan-body-first-principles-check ALREADY fires "before authoring tests, implementations, or doctrine prescribed by a plan body" — the exact moment, with three clauses that are all "is this authoring-artefact well-formed"; outcome/sequencing optionality are cohesive siblings; (c) separate rule files would duplicate one trigger across N always-on surfaces, erode the 80k always-loaded budget, and fragment a cohesive check — worse architecture even with infinite budget. The steelman for dedicated rules (salience) fails: salience comes from firing at the moment, which a 4th clause does identically.
3. **No new always-on rule files** → so WS-Z will add NO RULES_INDEX rows on Lane E's behalf (confirm back if you diverge).

This is the shape; the AUTHORING is yours (per `new-rule-vs-pdr-clause`) and lands under owner review of your diff. You don't need to re-derive the home decision — re-confirm the PDR-058 line references against the live file and write it. Report back to Shaded on this thread when it lands + is owner-approved.
